text.title: Security as Code (Policy as Code) Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Kyverno reports 200 violations/day; nobody fixes them; same violations recur.
antipattern.reason: Policies in audit mode 'to avoid breaking the team'; no enforcement deadline.
antipattern.note: Fix: Promote to enforce with a 2-week notice. Issue waivers for legacy workloads with 90-day expiry. Track waiver burn-down weekly.

antipattern.description: CI runs Trivy on every build; finds 50 criticals; merges anyway.
antipattern.note: Fix: Set Trivy job to fail-on critical. Require security-team override label to merge with criticals (documented exception).

antipattern.description: Cluster pulls images from a public registry without verification; SLSA review finds no build provenance.
antipattern.reason: Signing infra never set up; admission controller absent.
antipattern.note: Fix: Install cosign in build pipeline; sign every image. Install Sigstore policy-controller; require signature for the prod namespace.

antipattern.description: Production Kyverno policies edited via kubectl edit; no git history.
antipattern.reason: Team treated policies as one-off configs.
antipattern.note: Fix: Move all policies to git. Apply via ArgoCD / Flux. Lock the policy controller against console edits via RBAC.

antipattern.description: Post-breach forensics: cannot determine which images ran, which admission decisions allowed them.
antipattern.reason: Admission webhook ran without log shipping.
antipattern.note: Fix: Configure admission-controller audit log shipping to a separate-account SIEM. Replay 30 days of events; close gaps.
